---
title: "video_game_tidyverse.R"
author: "Joshua Henry"
date: "2026-04-12"
output: html_document
---

games <- read.csv("video_games.csv")
games <- as_tibble(games)
glimpse(games)

games_clean <- games %>%
  drop_na()
  
##Global Sales by Genre
genre_sales <- games_clean %>%
  group_by(Genre) %>%
  summarise(total_sales = sum(Global_Sales)) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_sales))

genre_sales

ggplot(genre_sales, aes(x = reorder(Genre, total_sales), y = total_sales)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Global Sales by Genre",
       x = "Genre",
       y = "Sales")
       
##Global Sales by Platform
platform_sales <- games_clean %>%
  group_by(Platform) %>%
  summarise(total_sales = sum(Global_Sales)) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_sales))

platform_sales

ggplot(platform_sales, aes(x = reorder(Platform, total_sales), y = total_sales)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Global Sales by Platform",
       x = "Platform",
       y = "Sales")
       
##Sales Over Time
year_sales <- games_clean %>%
  group_by(Year) %>%
  summarise(total_sales = sum(Global_Sales))

year_sales

ggplot(year_sales, aes(x = Year, y = total_sales)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title = "Sales Over Time",
       x = "Year",
       y = "Global Sales")
       

---

# 🔹 README

```md
## Tidyverse Video Game Sales Analysis

This vignette demonstrates how to use Tidyverse tools such as dplyr and ggplot2 to analyze video game sales data.

### Features:
- Data cleaning using drop_na()
- Grouping and summarizing data
- Visualization using ggplot2

###Dataset:
Video game sales dataset including global and regional sales.

# --- EXTENSION BY CHINGIZ RABDANOV ---
# This section adds a practical analysis of market share by genre.
# It helps identify which game genres have the highest sales impact.

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

# 1. Prepare the data: Filter out missing genres and calculate total sales
genre_sales <- df %>%
  filter(!is.na(Genre)) %>%
  group_by(Genre) %>%
  summarise(
    Total_Global_Sales = sum(Global_Sales, na.rm = TRUE),
    Average_Sales = mean(Global_Sales, na.rm = TRUE),
    Game_Count = n()
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Total_Global_Sales))

# 2. Visualize the Top 10 Genres by Total Sales
# This is crucial for business decisions in game development.
ggplot(head(genre_sales, 10), aes(x = reorder(Genre, Total_Global_Sales), y = Total_Global_Sales)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() + # Makes labels easier to read
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Top 10 Video Game Genres by Global Sales",
    subtitle = "Data-driven insight into market dominance",
    x = "Genre",
    y = "Total Sales (Millions)"
  )
